title: AaaS root hygiene remediation protocol
principle: Detect aggressively, remediate safely. AaaS may plan and verify root hygiene cleanup; it may only mutate artifacts after each forbidden root is classified.

appliesTo: fleet repos carrying AaaS contract pins

lifecycle.description: List forbidden live roots, classify file counts, hashes, git status, references, and likely canonical homes.
Assign every forbidden root to one remediation class before mutation.
Apply only Class R actions: allowlist legitimate root files with rationale, archive generated/historical material, move local transient material.
Open handoffs for source-of-record migration, destructive deletion, or ambiguous legacy roots.
Re-run root hygiene, QASC, AaaS contract, and SHIP gates; close handoff items only when evidence clears.

allowlist-with-rationale.allowedWhen: The root artifact is a legitimate repo-level control file and the canonical allowlist omitted it.

archive-generated-or-historical.allowedWhen: The material is historical, generated, or no longer source-of-record but must remain recoverable.

move-local-transient.allowedWhen: The material is local-only, non-secret runtime output or developer scratch state.

source-of-record-migration.allowedWhen: The root contains canonical product, architecture, delivery, or operational source material.

destructive-delete.allowedWhen: Deletion would remove tracked source material or legal/compliance evidence.

ambiguous-legacy-root.allowedWhen: The root may contain mixed historical and source-of-record material.

releasePolicy.qasc: Root hygiene failure is a QASC blocker until resolved or allowlisted with evidence.
releasePolicy.dslc: Release-management cannot mark DSLC passed when ambiguous root hygiene handoffs remain open for the release candidate.
releasePolicy.ship: SHIP requires root hygiene clean, evidence fresh, and public docs/changelog paths free of legacy root drift.
